items.headline: Sveltejs devalue's `devalue.parse` and `devalue.unflatten` emit objects with `__proto__` own properties

content.excerpt_text: In some circumstances, `devalue.parse` and `devalue.unflatten` could emit objects with `__proto__` own properties. This in and of itself is not a security vulnerability (and is possible with, for example, `JSON.parse` as well), but it can result in prototype injection if _downstream_ code handles it incorrectly: ```ts const result = devalue.parse(/* input creating an object with a __proto__ property */); const target = {}; Object.assign(target, result); // target's prototype is now polluted ```

items.headline: Keycloak: Information disclosure of disabled user attributes via administrative endpoint

content.excerpt_text: A flaw was found in Keycloak. An authenticated user with the view-users role could exploit a vulnerability in the UserResource component. items.headline: Parse Server's MFA recovery codes not consumed after use

content.excerpt_text: ### Impact When multi-factor authentication (MFA) via TOTP is enabled for a user account, Parse Server generates two single-use recovery codes. These codes are intended as a fallback when the user cannot provide a TOTP token. However, recovery codes are not consumed after use, allowing the same recovery code to be used an unlimited number of times. This defeats the single-use design of recovery codes and weakens the security of MFA-protected accounts. An attacker who obtains a single...
